merged_findings.finding: Phase 1 states 'the repo-local scripts/dispatch-bench-run.sh wrapper (created in Phase 3) handles prompt-file reading entirely on the repo side and pipes the content to the unchanged global script via stdin.' Phase 3 explicitly states 'No wrapper script is needed; no changes to the global dispatcher are needed' and instead passes the prompt as a positional argv argument directly to dispatch-to-node.sh. Phase 3's file list does not include scripts/dispatch-bench-run.sh. These are contradictory prompt-delivery mechanisms (stdin pipe vs positional arg) and the wrapper file is never created.

claude.finding: Phase 1 states 'the repo-local scripts/dispatch-bench-run.sh wrapper (created in Phase 3) handles prompt-file reading entirely on the repo side and pipes the content to the unchanged global script via stdin.' Phase 3 explicitly states 'No wrapper script is needed; no changes to the global dispatcher are needed' and instead passes the prompt as a positional argv argument directly to dispatch-to-node.sh. Phase 3's file list does not include scripts/dispatch-bench-run.sh. These are contradictory prompt-delivery mechanisms (stdin pipe vs positional arg) and the wrapper file is never created.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 4 lists Below local Qwen-2.5-Coder as the paraphraser fallback, but the only verified Below endpoint is POST /api/classify (a classifier). Paraphrasing requires text generation (spec rewriting), not classification. No text generation endpoint on Below (e.g. Ollama /api/generate or /api/chat) is verified in the dependency table. The paraphraser fallback path has no confirmed API to call.

claude.finding: Phase 4 lists Below local Qwen-2.5-Coder as the paraphraser fallback, but the only verified Below endpoint is POST /api/classify (a classifier). Paraphrasing requires text generation (spec rewriting), not classification. No text generation endpoint on Below (e.g. Ollama /api/generate or /api/chat) is verified in the dependency table. The paraphraser fallback path has no confirmed API to call.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 2 deliverables create core/benchmark/lockres.py (NEW, created in this phase) but the file is missing from Phase 2's file list. Phase 3 imports lockres.py and lists Phase 2 as a blocker based on it. An implementer following only the file list would miss creating this file.

claude.finding: Phase 2 deliverables create core/benchmark/lockres.py (NEW, created in this phase) but the file is missing from Phase 2's file list. Phase 3 imports lockres.py and lists Phase 2 as a blocker based on it. An implementer following only the file list would miss creating this file.

merged_findings.finding: Repository Preconditions state 'Phase 2 creates tests/benchmark/__init__.py (NEW)' but this file does not appear in any phase's file list. Without it, Python test discovery for tests/benchmark/test_selector.py and test_api_clients.py may fail depending on test runner configuration.

claude.finding: Repository Preconditions state 'Phase 2 creates tests/benchmark/__init__.py (NEW)' but this file does not appear in any phase's file list. Without it, Python test discovery for tests/benchmark/test_selector.py and test_api_clients.py may fail depending on test runner configuration.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 9 success criteria states 'All 7 x 2 x 3 x 2 = 84 runs produce valid RunResult rows' but the same deliverable says 5-6 surviving candidates after paraphrase filtering is acceptable. If only 5 survive, there are 60 runs, not 84. The success criteria contradicts the degraded-N handling.

claude.finding: Phase 9 success criteria states 'All 7 x 2 x 3 x 2 = 84 runs produce valid RunResult rows' but the same deliverable says 5-6 surviving candidates after paraphrase filtering is acceptable. If only 5 survive, there are 60 runs, not 84. The success criteria contradicts the degraded-N handling.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 4 deliverable specifies 'an offline calibration set of 20 hand-labeled (original, paraphrase) pairs (10 true-equivalent, 10 true-divergent)' for Below calibration, but no phase is responsible for producing these labeled pairs. Who creates them, when, and from what source material is unspecified.

claude.finding: Phase 4 deliverable specifies 'an offline calibration set of 20 hand-labeled (original, paraphrase) pairs (10 true-equivalent, 10 true-divergent)' for Below calibration, but no phase is responsible for producing these labeled pairs. Who creates them, when, and from what source material is unspecified.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 3 passes full prompt text as a positional command-line argument (argv[5]) to dispatch-to-node.sh, which is an unmodified shell script whose internal handling of $4 is not shown. If the script uses unquoted variable expansion, eval, or passes the argument through another shell layer, prompts containing shell metacharacters will break despite subprocess.Popen(shell=False) on the Python side.

claude.finding: Phase 3 passes full prompt text as a positional command-line argument (argv[5]) to dispatch-to-node.sh, which is an unmodified shell script whose internal handling of $4 is not shown. If the script uses unquoted variable expansion, eval, or passes the argument through another shell layer, prompts containing shell metacharacters will break despite subprocess.Popen(shell=False) on the Python side.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 1 creates requirements-benchmark.txt with 'full pin list (see Phase 5 file list for the canonical contents)' and scripts/install-benchmark-deps.sh to install them. Phase 5 creates scripts/install-benchmark-tools.sh which also runs 'python -m venv .venv-benchmark && .venv-benchmark/bin/pip install -r requirements-benchmark.txt'. The venv creation and pip install are duplicated across two scripts with unclear ownership.

claude.finding: Phase 1 creates requirements-benchmark.txt with 'full pin list (see Phase 5 file list for the canonical contents)' and scripts/install-benchmark-deps.sh to install them. Phase 5 creates scripts/install-benchmark-tools.sh which also runs 'python -m venv .venv-benchmark && .venv-benchmark/bin/pip install -r requirements-benchmark.txt'. The venv creation and pip install are duplicated across two scripts with unclear ownership.

merged_findings.finding: The plan declares model names Codex 5.4 and Claude Opus 4.7, but training_cutoffs.py lists cutoff dates as plan constants without a verification mechanism. If the actual model IDs or cutoff dates differ at execution time, the stratification analysis is silently wrong. No preflight check validates model identity against the dispatch runtime.

claude.finding: The plan declares model names Codex 5.4 and Claude Opus 4.7, but training_cutoffs.py lists cutoff dates as plan constants without a verification mechanism. If the actual model IDs or cutoff dates differ at execution time, the stratification analysis is silently wrong. No preflight check validates model identity against the dispatch runtime.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 6 de-blinding classifier uses an 80/20 split on pilot data from Phase 9, but the pilot has only 84 runs (42 pairs). An 80/20 split yields ~8 held-out pairs, not the 20 stated in Phase 6 deliverables. The 20-pair holdout requirement cannot be met from the pilot sample size.

claude.finding: Phase 6 de-blinding classifier uses an 80/20 split on pilot data from Phase 9, but the pilot has only 84 runs (42 pairs). An 80/20 split yields ~8 held-out pairs, not the 20 stated in Phase 6 deliverables. The 20-pair holdout requirement cannot be met from the pilot sample size.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 4's paraphraser fallback is fabricated: it says the fallback paraphraser is 'Below local Qwen-2.5-Coder', but the only verified Below contract in the cited material is `POST /api/classify` returning `{category, confidence}`. No text-generation/paraphrasing endpoint or local generation API is cited for Below.

codex.finding: Phase 4's paraphraser fallback is fabricated: it says the fallback paraphraser is 'Below local Qwen-2.5-Coder', but the only verified Below contract in the cited material is `POST /api/classify` returning `{category, confidence}`. No text-generation/paraphrasing endpoint or local generation API is cited for Below.

merged_findings.finding: The Lomax integration is unsafe under the plan's parallel execution model. The verified Lomax API only exposes `POST /api/projects/{project}/build` and `GET /api/projects/{project}/build/status` with no build-specific or ref-specific status endpoint, yet the benchmark runs many parallel worktrees of the same project slug and reads verdicts from project-level status. Without an explicit global serialization lock around Lomax builds, statuses can be overwritten and attributed to the wrong run.

codex.finding: The Lomax integration is unsafe under the plan's parallel execution model. The verified Lomax API only exposes `POST /api/projects/{project}/build` and `GET /api/projects/{project}/build/status` with no build-specific or ref-specific status endpoint, yet the benchmark runs many parallel worktrees of the same project slug and reads verdicts from project-level status. Without an explicit global serialization lock around Lomax builds, statuses can be overwritten and attributed to the wrong run.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 1 and Phase 3 contradict each other about dispatching. Phase 1 says Phase 3 will create a repo-local `scripts/dispatch-bench-run.sh` wrapper to handle prompt-file reading, while Phase 3 later says no wrapper script is needed and the prompt is passed directly as an argv positional argument.

codex.finding: Phase 1 and Phase 3 contradict each other about dispatching. Phase 1 says Phase 3 will create a repo-local `scripts/dispatch-bench-run.sh` wrapper to handle prompt-file reading, while Phase 3 later says no wrapper script is needed and the prompt is passed directly as an argv positional argument.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 6 makes format normalization mandatory before judging, but it never defines a failure path for invalid contestant outputs. Prettier, Black, and `ruff --fix` will fail on malformed `.ts/.js/.json/.py/...` files, so broken model outputs can crash the judge pipeline instead of being scored as low-quality runs.

codex.finding: Phase 6 makes format normalization mandatory before judging, but it never defines a failure path for invalid contestant outputs. Prettier, Black, and `ruff --fix` will fail on malformed `.ts/.js/.json/.py/...` files, so broken model outputs can crash the judge pipeline instead of being scored as low-quality runs.

merged_findings.finding: The de-blinding gate is internally inconsistent. Phase 6 requires a held-out set of 20 pairs for the classifier acceptance test, but Phase 9's pilot is only 7 phases, which yields at most 14 phase×sub-study comparison pairs. The stated acceptance set cannot be formed from the pilot as written.

codex.finding: The de-blinding gate is internally inconsistent. Phase 6 requires a held-out set of 20 pairs for the classifier acceptance test, but Phase 9's pilot is only 7 phases, which yields at most 14 phase×sub-study comparison pairs. The stated acceptance set cannot be formed from the pilot as written.

merged_findings.finding: Phase-drop handling is incomplete. Phase 1 says dropped phases are filtered by `is_phase_dropped(phase_id)` in the aggregator and reporter, but the runner/resume path is only specified to skip completed `run_id`s. Nothing says the executor stops launching remaining cells for a phase after a drop tombstone is written, so dropped phases can keep consuming runs and corrupt balance.

codex.finding: Phase-drop handling is incomplete. Phase 1 says dropped phases are filtered by `is_phase_dropped(phase_id)` in the aggregator and reporter, but the runner/resume path is only specified to skip completed `run_id`s. Nothing says the executor stops launching remaining cells for a phase after a drop tombstone is written, so dropped phases can keep consuming runs and corrupt balance.
